fragility_points: Need for exact contract-window timezone handling.
Could be disrupted by an unusual resolution dispute.
key_assumptions: Binance API minute data matches the operative Binance chart data referenced by Polymarket.
The ET qualifying window was mapped correctly in verification.
No settlement exception invalidates the observed >2400 print.
main_logical_chain: Identify the governing source of truth from Polymarket rules.
Verify whether Binance ETH/USDT printed any qualifying 1-minute high in the contract window.
Observed 2415.5 high implies the trigger already appears satisfied.
Residual uncertainty is operational settlement risk, not further ETH upside risk.
main_thesis: The Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute high appears to have already exceeded $2,400 within the qualifying window, leaving mostly settlement-path risk rather than price-path risk.
quote_anchors: any Binance 1-minute candle ... High price equal to or greater than the price specified
source_quality_view: High quality overall: Polymarket provides the contract wording and Binance provides direct venue data, with low remaining source-of-truth ambiguity after verification.
strongest_disconfirmers: Residual risk that Binance API output differs from the exact chart/UI Polymarket expects.
Small possibility of timezone/window interpretation error or settlement dispute.
strongest_supports: Polymarket rules say any Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute candle high >= 2400 in the Apr 13-19 ET window resolves Yes.
Binance verification found a 1-minute high of 2415.5 on 2026-04-14 10:32 ET.
The exceedance is $15.5 above threshold, reducing edge-case rounding risk.
timing_relevance: High; this is a date-specific threshold market, but the timing risk collapsed once a qualifying high appears to have already printed on Apr 14.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether the Binance chart UI would show any discrepancy versus API data.
Why assignment metadata still showed 0.916 while later page data showed 1.0.
what_would_change_view: A documented mismatch between Binance UI and API, an ET window error, or an official Polymarket dispute/exception would reduce confidence materially; archived confirmation from Binance or Polymarket would push confidence to effectively 100%.
